examples: Studying takes up most of my time at the moment.

phrasalVerbs.definition: destroy or remove (for buildings, statues, etc)
examples: That beautiful old building was torn down to make way for a new supermarket.
Robin suddenly grabbed the letter out of my hand and tore it up.
When I asked Theresa to marry me, she said she’d have to think it over.
The CD wouldn’t play any more, so I threw it away.

phrasalVerbs.definition: put on (a piece of clothing) to see how it looks and whether it fits
examples: Debra took the skirt into the changing room to try it on.
England need to try out a few new players before the World Cup starts.
I applied to join the Air Force, but they turned me down because of my eyesight.
It started as the holiday of a lifetime but soon turned into a nightmare.
